Done. I audited the IVX AI chat end-to-end, fixed the root cause, and deployed the update.

**What I fixed:**
- `expo/app/ivx/chat.tsx`
- Added a `pendingInitialScrollRef` so the chat knows it must anchor to the newest message on first open / conversation switch.
- Added `scrollToBottomRobust()` that retries `scrollToEnd` and falls back to `scrollToIndex(lastItem)` across multiple timing checkpoints (80/220/260/500/620/1200 ms) so it works even when FlatList hasn't finished measuring dynamic message bubbles.
- Updated `FlatList` `onLayout` and `onContentSizeChange` to use the pending flag, so later layout checkpoints finish the anchor if the first scroll misses.
- Stopped the unconditional `scrollToEnd` on every content-size change that could fight with the user when reading older messages.

**Why the chat was "disappearing":**
`scrollToEnd` was firing before the list had measured its content. It silently failed, leaving the latest messages below the fold. The chat looked blank/disappeared because the newest conversation was off-screen.
